title: Change Dictionary Items
content:
### Changing Dictionary Items

---

### Update a Value by Key

```python
student = {"name": "Alice", "age": 25}
student["age"] = 26  # Update existing key
print(student)  # {"name": "Alice", "age": 26}
```

---

### `.update()` to Change Multiple

```python
student = {"name": "Alice", "age": 25, "grade": "B"}
student.update({"grade": "A", "age": 26})
print(student)  # {"name": "Alice", "age": 26, "grade": "A"}
```

---

### Conditional Update

```python
student = {"name": "Alice", "age": 25}

if student["age"] < 30:
    student["level"] = "Junior"
else:
    student["level"] = "Senior"
```

---

### Merge with `|` Operator (Python 3.9+)

```python
dict1 = {"a": 1, "b": 2}
dict2 = {"b": 3, "c": 4}
merged = dict1 | dict2  # dict2 overwrites dict1
print(merged)  # {"a": 1, "b": 3, "c": 4}
```

---

> **Note:** When you assign to an existing key, the old value is **overwritten**. There is no warning.
codeExample:
student = {"name": "Alice", "age": 25, "grade": "B"}

# Change single value
student["grade"] = "A"
print("Changed grade:", student)

# Change multiple
student.update({"age": 26, "name": "Alice Smith"})
print("After update:", student)

# Merge operator
d1 = {"x": 1, "y": 2}
d2 = {"y": 3, "z": 4}
print("Merged:", d1 | d2)

quiz.question: What happens if you assign to an existing dictionary key?
options: It creates a new entry with a different key
